codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly rejects memories with cosine distance < 0.15 to existing memories in the same repository. Code is well-structured with proper error handling, repository isolation, and comprehensive test coverage.

issues.description: The `update` method does not call `find_similar_memory`, so updating an entry's embedding to something similar to another entry bypasses the similarity check. This may be intentional since the feature describes 'memories' (new entries), but if strict deduplication is required for all embedding changes, `update` should also check similarity. The `find_similar_memory` method already has self-skip logic suggesting it was intended for updates.

Race condition: Two concurrent `store` calls with similar embeddings could both pass the `find_similar_memory` check before either writes, since there's no transaction isolation around the check-then-insert. For MVP this is acceptable, but production would need row-level locking or transactions.

The expectedBehavior field says 'Reject if cosine distance < 0.85 (similarity > 0.15)' which contradicts the feature description 'cosine distance 0.15'. The implementation correctly uses 0.15 threshold, so this appears to be a typo in features.json.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The expectedBehavior comment in features.json appears to contain a typo ('cosine distance < 0.85' instead of 'cosine distance < 0.15'). While the implementation is correct, this inconsistency could confuse future developers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json expectedBehavior[1]: 'Reject if cosine distance < 0.85 (similarity > 0.15)' vs feature description: 'Reject memories within cosine distance 0.15'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill was followed correctly. No deviations noted.
No services or external commands were used beyond standard cargo commands. No gap identified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification used only cargo build, cargo test, cargo fmt, cargo clippy

summary: Feature mem-similarity-check passes review. Implementation correctly rejects memories with cosine distance < 0.15 to existing memories in the same repository. Error variant SimilarMemoryFound(uuid::Uuid) properly added to SwellError. 9 similarity tests pass. Repository isolation verified. Non-blocking observations: update() doesn't check similarity (may be intentional), race condition on concurrent stores (acceptable for MVP), features.json expectedBehavior typo.
